output.final_deliverable:
Visit performed: https://github.com/python/cpython (source_url in visit result)

Extracted repository metadata (from the visited page content provided):

1) Primary programming language
   - Extracted value: MISSING in provided visit results
   - Evidence checked: visit result fields (content, page_title, h1_text, links). No explicit "Primary language" or language name present in the merged visit result.
   - Citation: https://github.com/python/cpython

2) Number of stars (approximate)
   - Extracted value: MISSING in provided visit results
   - Evidence checked: visit result fields do not contain a star count or star text (e.g., "stars", "k", or numeric value).
   - Citation: https://github.com/python/cpython

3) License type
   - Extracted value: MISSING in provided visit results
   - Evidence checked: visit result fields do not contain license text (e.g., "MIT", "PSF", "Apache-2.0").
   - Citation: https://github.com/python/cpython

4) Brief repository description
   - Extracted value: "The Python programming language"
   - Source field: page_title from the visit result is exactly: "GitHub - python/cpython: The Python programming language"
   - Exact text used: The Python programming language
   - Citation: https://github.com/python/cpython

Other visit metadata (from the merged visit result):
- page_title: "GitHub - python/cpython: The Python programming language"
  - Citation: https://github.com/python/cpython
- h1_text: "Search code, repositories, users, issues, pull requests..."
  - Citation: https://github.com/python/cpython
- content (raw snippet): "https://support.github.com?tags=dotcom-footer"
  - Citation: https://github.com/python/cpython
- links_count: 20 (list of links included in visit result)
  - Citation: https://github.com/python/cpython

Notes about missing data:
- The visit result provided in the merged results did not include the repository's primary language label, the displayed star count, or license text. Because those specific fields were not present in the provided visit output, I cannot invent or assume values — they are reported above as MISSING.

If you want, I can re-visit the same URL and attempt to capture the missing fields (primary language, star count, license) and return exact values with citations. Current deliverable uses only the evidence present in the merged visit results.
output.action_summary: I visited https://github.com/python/cpython and extracted what was present in the provided visit result. The page_title gives the brief description: "The Python programming language." The primary programming language, star count (approximate), and license type were not present in the merged visit result and are therefore reported as MISSING. Source: https://github.com/python/cpython

merged_results.title: Analyze visited page content and normalize extracted fields (stars number, license verification, brief summary)

result.title: Analyze visited page content and normalize extracted fields (stars number, license verification, brief summary)

details.intent: Take the raw outputs from the visit node and (1) normalize the star count into an approximate integer, (2) verify the license by checking the license_file_url or LICENSE file contents if present in the visit output, (3) produce a concise repository summary combining the description and primary language, and (4) prepare a final, verifiable output object with explicit fields.
inputs_needed_from_children: page_content and extracted raw fields from the visit node (primary_language, stars_text, license_type, description, license_file_url, source_url)
details.normalization_plan: Convert stars_text to stars_approx_int: remove commas, handle suffixes like 'k' (multiply by 1,000) and 'M' (multiply by 1,000,000), round to a sensible precision (nearest hundred or thousand depending on magnitude) and mark as approximate. For license verification: if license_file_url available, check that it points to a GitHub license file or recognized SPDX identifier; if the visit content included the LICENSE text, verify the license name found in file header matches the license_type label.
details.verification_steps: 1) Confirm numeric conversion by reversing the conversion and comparing to original stars_text. 2) If license_file_url is absent, try to find a LICENSE file link in the visited page content before finalizing license_type. 3) If primary_language is ambiguous, pick the language that the repository UI marks as the top (largest portion) and note ambiguity in the output.
measurable_output_fields: summary (string) - one-sentence summary combining description and primary language
details.rationale: Normalization and verification ensure the output is a clean, machine-usable summary with traceable evidence back to the page content. This reduces uncertainty about numeric formatting and license identification.
details.goal: Analyze visited page content and normalize extracted fields (stars number, license verification, brief summary)
details.original_goal: Analyze visited page content and normalize extracted fields (stars number, license verification, brief summary)

a5d37d9f-e828-4789-8ea8-62bfebcfc485.title: Visit the CPython GitHub repository page (https://github.com/python/cpython) and extract repository metadata

details.intent: Directly load the CPython GitHub repository page and extract the repository's primary programming language, star count (raw text and normalized approximate integer), license type, and the brief repository description. The user mandate requires visiting the GitHub page directly; do not rely on search results.
target_facts: primary_language (string) - language shown in repo UI / language bar
stars_text (string) - exact text shown in the Stargazers button, e.g. '52k' or '53,000'
license_type (string) - license label shown in repository header/about box (e.g., 'PSF License', 'MIT', 'BSD-3-Clause')
description (string) - short repository description from the About box or page header
license_file_url (string, if present) - exact URL to license file/link on GitHub
details.extraction_plan: Load the exact URL. Parse the repository page DOM and extract these elements: (1) the language badge or language bar (About/Repository metadata) for primary_language; (2) the 'Stargazers' button/link text (selector: link ending with '/stargazers' or visible star count in header) for stars_text; (3) the license label in the repository metadata or the 'License' link for license_type and license_file_url; (4) the short description shown under the repo name or the 'About' section for description. Store the raw extracted strings and the page URL. If the page presents stars with shorthand (e.g., '54.1k'), record raw text and convert to integer in a downstream step.
details.verification_steps: 1) Confirm that the license link (if present) points to a GitHub LICENSE file or a recognized license identifier. 2) Ensure the stargazers link leads to the '/stargazers' path. 3) If multiple language badges appear, choose the one marked as the primary language in the repo's language summary. 4) Save the exact extracted text values to allow later re-checking.
details.rationale: This visit is mandatory per the user's instruction to open the GitHub page directly. Extracting the raw UI values allows downstream normalization (e.g., converting 'k' to integers) and verification by checking links to the license file and stargazers page.
details.goal: Visit the CPython GitHub repository page (https://github.com/python/cpython) and extract repository metadata
details.original_goal: Visit the CPython GitHub repository page (https://github.com/python/cpython) and extract repository metadata

You must visit the GitHub page directly - do not rely on search results.
